questions.question: What happens if you declare a field as `static final` in a class?
options: It becomes an instance constant unique to each object
It becomes a class-level constant shared by all instances
It can only be accessed inside the class
It must be initialized in a constructor
questions.explanation: A static final field is a class-level constant — one copy shared across all instances. By convention, it's named in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

questions.question: Which statement about the `this` keyword is FALSE?
options: It can be used to call another constructor in the same class
It refers to the current object instance
It can be used in a static method
It can disambiguate field names from parameter names
questions.explanation: The `this` keyword cannot be used in static methods because static methods belong to the class, not to any instance.

questions.question: What is the result of `new Account() == new Account()` where Account has a no-arg constructor?
options: true — both are Account objects
false — == compares references, and each `new` creates a different object
Compile error — cannot use == with objects
Depends on the equals() implementation
questions.explanation: The == operator compares object references. Each `new` creates a distinct object on the heap, so two different references are never ==.

questions.question: Which nested class type has an implicit reference to its enclosing instance?
options: Static nested class
Inner class (non-static member class)
Neither — nested classes never reference enclosing instances
Both — all nested classes hold a reference
questions.explanation: A non-static inner class holds an implicit reference to the enclosing object (Outer.this), while a static nested class does not.

questions.question: What happens if you don't initialize a `final` instance field?
options: It gets a default value (0, null, false)
Compile error — final fields must be initialized in declaration or constructor
It throws a NullPointerException at runtime
The JVM initializes it automatically
questions.explanation: A final instance field must be definitely assigned by the end of every constructor. If not, the compiler reports an error.
